reasoning: To mark the objective "terminate the Public Service Loan Forgiveness program" as "complete" on the Project 2025 Tracker, there would need to be a formal termination of the program. Because PSLF was created by Congress, it requires an act of Congress to terminate. While Trump signed an Executive Order on March 7, 2025 limiting some eligibility, this does not terminate the program. A House committee passed the "Student Success and Taxpayer Savings Plan" in late April 2025, which might restrict or de facto eliminate PSLF by repealing IDR plans, but this legislation is only at the committee stage as of early May. Passing a massive budget reconciliation bill through the House and Senate generally takes several months, making passage before July 1, 2025 highly unlikely. Furthermore, even if passed, the draft proposals suggest a phased elimination rather than an immediate termination, which the Tracker is unlikely to classify as fully "complete" in that timeframe. Therefore, the objective will almost certainly remain "in progress" or "partial" by the resolution date.
